branch: agent/juno-code-remote-backend (PR #19; now contains all of PR #18's agent/juno-native-claude-continuation)
currentPhase: pr18-merged-into-pr19; next is the migration correction, then the Stage-4 orphan triage, then release integration
currentTask: PR #18 was merged into PR #19 rather than rebased, so the four PR #19 commits (8dd231c, 0719f59, d08198e, cedc264) keep their identities and no force-push is needed. The merge had exactly one conflict, docs/native/handoff.json, resolved here; docs/native/API_GAPS.md auto-merged. Verified after the merge: the native and backend contracts both still read 1.3.0 (PR #19's bump wins over PR #18's 1.2.0), and all 27 OpenAPI paths survive including the four /code ones. IMPORTANT AND NOT YET DONE: prisma/migrations/20260721120000_backfill_entity_revisions/migration.sql on this branch is still the bare-NULL form (0 typed NULL::timestamp, must be 22) — it has to be taken verbatim from origin/main before any release. Stage-4 finding: the orphaned backend work sitting uncommitted on the main checkout implements /api/code/devices/{deviceId}/sessions/** — a session-relay surface that is NOT in the published contract at all. PR #19 published only the READ surface of the ALREADY EXISTING task-based control plane and changed no backend route. So the orphan is a candidate implementation of a different, unpublished surface, and porting it wholesale would ship un-contracted routes. BLOCKED, NEEDS THE OWNER: all macOS visual QA. Screen Recording is not granted to the controlling process (a full-screen screencapture returns pure black; -l<winid> and -R<rect> are refused outright), and every Xcode-built Juno target launches, runs a normal event loop and creates ZERO windows — reproduced on pre-redesign commit 2075a5c, so it is NOT a redesign regression, while a hand-built minimal SwiftUI app in the same directory does get a window. The earlier 'killall cfprefsd broke window creation' diagnosis is wrong: TextEdit launches fine in this same session.
